title: Darth Talon
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] Order|[[Sarasu Taalon]], a [[High Lord (Circle of Lords)|High Lord]] from the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]]|linebreak=1}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Talon-Leg0.png]] |name=Darth Talon |homeworld=[[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] {{C|[[Lethan/Legends|Lethan]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]{{CSWECite|III|215|Talon, Darth}} |pronouns=She/her[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]] |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]][[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] |skin=Red {{C|with [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoo]]s}} |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[One Sith]] |masters=*[[Darth Ruyn]] *[[Darth Krayt]] |apprentices=[[Cade Skywalker]][[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]] }} {{Quote|I am your life. I am your death. You have passed your final test without flinching. I name you Darth Talon and you will now be one of my Hands—My own personal assassin. Henceforth, you will take orders only from me—report only to me.|Darth Krayt|}} '''Darth Talon''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Lethan/Legends|Lethan]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who became a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] in [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] in [[137 ABY]]. Talon was styled with black Sith [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoos]] covering her body including her head and [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]]; each tattoo had been earned in ritual combat and inscribed by Krayt himself. As a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], she was trained by a fellow Twi'lek Sith Lord named [[Darth Ruyn]], whom she later killed on Krayt's command with a swift [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitating]] strike of her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], shortly before ascending to the rank of a full-fledged [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in Krayt's Order. Recognizing her unshakeable loyalty, Krayt anointed Talon as one of his two [[Hand (One Sith)|Hands]], thus making her an extension of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] own will. In this capacity, Talon found herself working alongside her Hand counterpart, [[Darth Nihl]]. Talon was often tasked with her Master's most important missions, including the capture of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] fugitive [[Cade Skywalker]]. She attempted to get him to join the One sith. She tried to seduce him to the dark side along with sleeping with him to form a bond and stronger connection to the dark side. She aided his training in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the]] [[The Force/Legends|Force]], until her apprentice renounced the One Sith when he stabbed her with his father’s lightsaber. Talon always remained loyal to her lord, Darth Krayt, even during [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok's]] attempted takeover of the One Sith, aiding Krayt in his recovery and along with Nihl fighting off the Sith who stood between Krayt and his former [[Voice]], witnessing his return to power. When Darth Krayt finally fell during the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|Battle of Coruscant]], she entered hiding along with the rest of the One Sith, working from concealment to achieve their goals by less overt means. ==Biography== ===Training=== {{Quote|Apprentice—do you know why your Master had to die?''"
"''Because you ordered it, Master.|Darth Krayt and Darth Talon|}} [[File:TalonGetsBuzzed-SWL02.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Talon is anointed as a Sith by Darth Krayt in 137 ABY.]] The [[Lethan/Legends|Lethan]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Talon was a third-generation member of the [[One Sith]] who lived over a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] after the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], raised and [[Sith training|trained]] in the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], an ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] burial [[Planet/Legends|world]] that served as the secret location of the revived Sith Order. Under the tutelage of [[Darth Ruyn]], Talon's inherent [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] as a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] were honed in order to transform her into a [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith assassin]]. Psychologically, she was molded to be completely susceptible to the tenets of the One Sith. Her ultimate loyalty, however, belonged without question to her order's founder, [[Darth Krayt]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|emperor]] of a [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|new Sith Empire]]. On the verge of her training's completion, Talon was greatly immersed within the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Her final test of loyalty was to slay Ruyn in cold blood. Obedient to the will of Darth Krayt without question, the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] did not hesitate when she [[Death/Legends|killed]] her own [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]]. With her final rite of passage fulfilled, Talon was elevated to the rank of [[Hand (One Sith)|Hand]], thus making her one of the Emperor's most trusted assassins. ===Pursuit of a princess=== {{Quote|I am Darth Talon… I am my master's hand… I am your death.|Darth Talon to Wolf Sazen on Vendaxa|[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]]}} [[File:Darth_Talon_LECG.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Darth Talon dives from her [[Kybuck speeder bike|Kybuck]] [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] on Socorro.]] Darth Talon's first mission as a Hand was to locate and capture [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]] in order to lure out her father, the deposed [[Emperor/Legends|emperor]] [[Roan Fel]], from hiding. Talon traveled to the planet [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] where she discovered Fel, who had been hiding in the [[Imperial Mission]]. Although the Princess eluded Talon, her bodyguard and mentor, [[Imperial Knight]] [[Elke Vetter]] was defeated in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] by the Sith Twi'lek. After [[Dismemberment/Legends|amputating]] Vetter's hand and stabbing her leg during the [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]], Talon then [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] Vetter with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Talon discovered that Fel escaped with an Imperial Missionary named [[Astraal Vao]]. With no further use for the fallen Knight, Talon killed Vetter using [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] and continued her pursuit of Fel. However, just before Talon was able to apprehend her target, Fel and Vao escaped from Socorro by boarding the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-turned-[[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]].[[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]] ===Confrontation on Vendaxa=== {{Quote|My Master's patience is exhausted. Take the Princess. Darth Krayt will tear the location of her father's hiding place from her. Slay the rest—carrion for Vendaxa's scavengers.|Darth Talon|}} [[File:Talon_Vendaxa.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Talon confronts Marasiah Fel and her allies on Vendaxa.]] Talon pursued Fel and her companions to the planet [[Vendaxa/Legends|Vendaxa]], where they were joined by two more Jedi, [[Wolf Sazen]] and [[Shado Vao]]. In order to prevent another escape attempt, Talon sabotaged the ''Mynock's'' flight systems and then rigged it with explosives. Through the dark side, she rallied a herd of [[vanx]] to her side. The creatures, under Talon's influence, attacked the group in a frenzy. While Fel and her allies defended themselves against the beasts, Talon engaged in combat with Sazen and Vao and skillfully held her own against the two Jedi. After incapacitating Vao with a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force push]], she turned her full attention to Sazen, whom she recognized as the Jedi Master who lost his arm in a duel with [[Darth Nihl]] during the [[Massacre at Ossus|Massacre of]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. The Sith Lady unleashed a barrage of Force lightning on Sazen, but was prevented from killing him by Marasiah Fel. The Princess, still enraged over the death of Elke Vetter, attacked Talon in the hopes of ensuring that no one else would have to die for her. Talon was further caught by surprise when Cade Skywalker used the Force to hurl a pile of wreckage at her. The impact of the attack sent the Sith soaring through the underbrush of the nearby jungle. [[File:KraytTalon.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Darth Talon beside Emperor Darth Krayt]] Despite the injuries Talon sustained from Skywalker's assault, she ultimately survived and awaited reinforcements from her Master. Soon after the Imperial Knights [[Antares Draco]] and [[Ganner Krieg]] arrived on Vendaxa in an unauthorized attempt to rescue the Princess, the fighting resumed as Talon and her fellow Sith, including Darth Nihl, launched an all-out attack on the combined group of Jedi, [[Empire-in-exile|Imperials]] and bounty hunters.[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]] During the ensuing [[Skirmish on Vendaxa|skirmish]], Darth Talon dueled with Shado Vao. Although Marasiah Fel was severely wounded, Talon ultimately failed in her objective to acquire the Imperial Princess, once again due to the intervention of Cade Skywalker. As the ''Mynock'' departed from Vendaxa with the injured Fel and company, Talon attempted to [[Force jump/Legends|board]] the ship as well, only to be Force pushed out of the vessel by Antares Draco.[[Legacy (2006) 7|''Legacy'' (2006) 7]] After the debacle on Vendaxa, Darth Talon returned to Coruscant in defeat and expected to be executed for failure. But when she informed her Master about Cade Skywalker's involvement, Darth Krayt chose to spare his Hand's life. Instead, Talon was instructed to hunt down and acquire Skywalker, supposedly [[Skywalker family/Legends|the last scion]] of the infamous Sith Lord [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. ===Duel in the Temple of the Sith=== {{Quote|How do you like me, schutta?! Think you can take me now?!|Cade Skywalker to Darth Talon|[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]]}} [[File:Duelinthetempleofthesith.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Darth Talon duels with Cade Skywalker on Coruscant.]] Shortly after the incident on Vendaxa, Cade Skywalker journeyed to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] where he intended to rescue [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Hosk Trey'lis]]. Although Skywalker infiltrated the [[Temple of the Sith]] and released Trey'lis, the two Jedi were confronted by Darth Talon. Their escape route blocked, Skywalker and Trey'lis were forced to confront the Sith assassin. Talon incapacitated Trey'lis, but was unable to overcome Skywalker's defenses. The fight caused the renegade Jedi to channel the dark side of the Force, until he finally overpowered Talon in combat. Darth Nihl, who had been observing the duel from a distance, Force choked Skywalker before he could kill Talon. Despite being delivered from her imminent death, Talon resented the intervention of her fellow Hand. Regardless, Skywalker was subdued and rendered a prisoner of the One Sith. While [[Darth Maladi]] subjected him to [[Torture/Legends|torture]], Krayt prepared for the task of luring him over to the dark side and the cause of the Sith; a task that Talon had an instrumental role in. ===Training Cade Skywalker=== {{Quote|Darkness calls to darkness. It calls to you. You can feel it like a heart beating within this temple.|Darth Talon|[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]}} Shortly after Skywalker's capture, he was given a private audience with Darth Krayt, with Talon and [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] present. The Dark Lord revealed his former identity as A'Sharad Hett, a Knight of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|original Jedi Order]] and a [[Jedi General/Legends|veteran]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] He then recounted the events that influenced his fall to the dark side of the Force; the [[Order 66/Legends|betrayal]] and [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|near extermination]] of the Jedi, the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|first Galactic Empire]], and Hett's self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. [[File:Vambraces-Sith_cropped.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Darth Talon trains Cade Skywalker in the ways of the One Sith.]] The revelation of Krayt's past, however, did not affect Skywalker in the least. When the former Jedi mocked the Dark Lord, Talon angrily attacked him; only to be Force pushed against a wall. Skywalker used the opportunity to display another feature of his [[Dark transfer|unique healing ability]]: the power to reverse the effects of healing. As a result, he re-opened all of Talon's recently mended wounds, an act which nearly killed her. At Krayt's request, Skywalker repaired the damage that he inflicted on Talon. Krayt wasted no time in his bid to lure Cade Skywalker over to the dark side. He decided to pair Talon and Skywalker together as master and apprentice. Although she was unconvinced that her new acolyte had any intention of becoming a Sith, Talon nevertheless complied with her own Master's will. During their time together, she recalled the history of the One Sith, as well as how its philosophy was inspired by the teachings of [[XoXaan]]. She also tried to convince Skywalker that his destiny was inevitably tied to the dark side and the Sith. The two gradually became closer as their time together progressed, a result of the [[Force-bond/Legends|bond]] that developed between them due to a side effect of Skywalker's healing power. They presumably slept together, but Talon remained convinced that her apprentice was only feigning interest in the Sith. But Krayt ignored Talon's suspicions because of his belief that Skywalker would ultimately come to embrace the dark side, regardless of any kind of initial resistance. ===Second duel=== {{Quote|Something to ''karkin'' remember me by, ''schutta''.|Cade Skywalker to Darth Talon|[[Legacy (2006) 18|''Legacy'' (2006) 18]]}} [[File:DarthTalonStabbed.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Cade Skywalker stabs Darth Talon.]] Darth Talon and Darth Nihl were present when Darth Krayt issued Cade Skywalker with an ultimatum: kill Hosk Trey'lis or die. Skywalker, well aware of the fact that Krayt could not afford to kill his only hope for survival, refused to execute his fellow Jedi. Enraged, Krayt struck down Trey'lis with his own blade. The act of murder succeeded in causing a reaction in Skywalker, but the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Master [[Kol Skywalker]] prevented his full submission to the dark side. After witnessing the apparition of the long dead Jedi Master, Cade Skywalker took up his [[Kol Skywalker's lightsaber|father's lightsaber]], which had been kept for years as a trophy in Krayt's [[Darth Krayt's throne room|throne room]]. Darth Talon was the first to attack, only to be [[Shiak|impaled]] through the abdomen by her own apprentice. As a result, she was utterly incapacitated and unable to prevent Skywalker's escape from Coruscant.[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]] Afterward, she was forced to spend much time in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]] in order to recuperate from her near fatal wound.[[Legacy (2006) 23|''Legacy'' (2006) 23]] ===Had Abbadon=== After recovering, Talon joined Krayt, Nihl's replacement [[Darth Stryfe]] and Maladi to [[Had Abbadon]], where Skywalker was spotted. Talon dueled against Skywalker taunting him that he was closer to the dark side than he thought. The duel ended shortly before explosives were detonated by Jariah Syn, leaving her and the other Sith minions unconscious. Shortly after, the man she had pledged her life to, Darth Krayt, was killed by his most trusted servant, Darth Wyyrlok. ===Division=== Sometime after her recovery, Darth Wyyrlok assigned her to guard [[Darth Krayt's tomb]] and to let no one in. However, Darth Nihl, convinced by Darth Maladi, traveled to Korriban to inquire as to Darth Krayt's survival. Talon prevented him from entering the tomb but chose to follow him when Nihl claimed that Krayt himself had summoned him with his mind. However, Krayt's body had vanished and only his armor remained. This shocked both Talon and Nihl. Sometime later, however, Talon mysteriously disappeared from the Temple. Deep within Korriban's tombs, she had found a very much alive Krayt, who declared that his rebirth had begun. Darth Nihl later found her and attacked her, but Krayt intervened. He then led both Sith deeper into the tombs and showed them that he had been creating a [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|new breed of Sith]] whose devotion was completely unquestionable. He then used his magnified powers to reach out to everyone in the galaxy that had touched the dark side to tell them that he lived. ===Assault on Coruscant=== [[File:Talon_assault.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Talon and Nihl face off with Darth Wyyrlok's Sith allies.]] In [[138 ABY]], Talon accompanied Darth Krayt in an [[Assault on the Temple of the Sith|attack]] he orchestrated in revenge for Darth Wyyrlok's betrayal. At first, Krayt, alongside Darth Talon, Darth Nihl and two Sith troopers, was confronted by five Sith allied to Darth Wyyrlok; however, Krayt and his followers quickly cut through them. ===Finding the Jedi=== Talon was involved in [[Darth Havok]]'s plan to break his prisoner, [[Imperial Knight]] [[Antares Draco]]. He created a [[Mind trick/Legends|Force illusion]] about the day that Havok [[Rooftop duel|killed]] Empress Elliah Fel and nearly died at Draco's hand. As Draco relived the duel, Havok appealed to his guilt and claimed that he had been too weak to save her. Havok then projected another illusion onto Darth Talon, making her appear to be Marasiah Fel. He told Draco that Vao and Krieg had been killed during their escape from Korriban and that Marasiah had been recaptured; Talon planted a kiss on Draco's lips to promote the deception. When Havok threatened to kill her if Draco did not tell him any valuable information, Draco broke down and revealed the existence of a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Jedi Hidden Temple]] on [[Taivas]]. Talon left to relay the findings to Darth Krayt as Havok continued to torture Draco. Krayt was eager to face Cade Skywalker in combat again and bade Talon to present a broken Draco to him as a gift.[[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy — War'' 2]] Talon had Draco [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|frozen]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]][[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] and brought him to the Wheel, where Skywalker was meeting with Calixte, now an enemy of Krayt after being outed as Fel's spy. Talon infiltrated the ''Mynock'' and placed Draco in its cargo hold before briefly [[Skirmish on The Wheel (138 ABY)|dueling]] Skywalker and fleeing the space station. ===Defending the Dark Lord=== [[File:Talon_and_Nihl_defending_Krayt.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Talon, Darth Stryfe and Darth Nihl flank Darth Krayt in order to protect him.]] After their defeat at Taivas, the allies attacked Coruscant before the Sith could prepare to invade Bastion. A strike team led by Cade Skywalker including Wolf Sazen, Shado Vao, Morrigan Corde, Ganner Krieg, Azlyn Rae, and Sigel Dare infiltrated the Sith Temple on Coruscant. Darth Talon, Darth Stryfe, and other Sith were waiting to defend Darth Krayt. As the fight ensued, Talon engaged Skywalker in a duel. She tried to use the Force to defeat him, but was overpowered. When Skywalker was distracted by his master's death, she took advantage of the opportunity and escaped. Fearing for her master's life, Talon realized that Skywalker was more powerful than before and sought to warn Darth Krayt.[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]] Talon's efforts to protect her master, however, ended in failure. Skywalker and Krayt fought once more as the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|final battle of the]] [[Second Imperial Civil War|war]] raged on around them. After the young Jedi succeeded in killing the Dark Lord, the One Sith was left in complete disarray. As the Empire swiftly crumbled without its emperor, thus making way for the rise of the [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]], Darth Talon and the remaining Sith went into hiding across the galaxy in an effort to ensure that the One Sith would not die with its founder. According to the orders of Darth Nihl, the new Dark Lord, all Sith followers were tasked with infiltrating every planetary government in the galaxy in order to strike back against the Alliance and the Jedi from the shadows.[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]] ==Powers and abilities== [[File:Talon-mugshot.png|thumb|right|230px|Darth Talon of the Sith]] Talon's ability in combat was considerable. She literally and metaphorically "disarmed" Elke Vetter without taking any physical damage. Her skill at fighting was further exemplified by the fact that she was anointed as one of Darth Krayt's two Hands, as aforementioned. On Vendaxa, Darth Talon was able to outfight the two Jedi Shado Vao and Wolf Sazen simultaneously, showing her aptitude at lightsaber combat. Only a last-second move from the nearby Princess Fel prevented Talon from slaying Master Sazen. She was known to use acrobatic flips and [[Force jump/Legends|leaps]] in lightsaber combat, possibly making her a user of [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]] lightsaber combat. As she had often, if not always, fought one-handed, she may have had some application of [[Form II/Legends|Makashi]]. Her talents at fighting, as well as her rank of Hand, put her at a status greater than the majority of the Sith. [[File:TalonForceLightningSazen-Legacy2006-5.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Darth Talon uses Force lightning.]] Talon was also skilled in Force techniques such as [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], by which she executed Imperial Knight Elke Vetter. She was able to verbally [[Drain Knowledge|reveal]] vital information from Vetter by a Force technique where she summoned a cyan circle of light before her adversary. She even [[Animal bond|manipulated]] the minds of beasts to aid her in her battle against the Jedi. And she was once able to [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force push|Force push]] Shado Vao to the ground with such strength that he was unable to recover for some time. Talon had a Force-bond with Cade Skywalker, a result of being healed by him. Thus, she could sense his feelings with ease.[[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]] She could also [[Force sense/Legends|sense]] Darth Krayt's life force and release him from stasis, which she accomplished with the aid of Force lightning. Darth Talon was able to shadow a Princess as she was running away from the burning Imperial Mission that was her former hiding spot, indicating her skill at concealment. Whether it was physical, Force-affiliated, or a mixture of both was unknown. If Force-affiliated, this might be indicative of the rare talent of [[Force cloak|Force concealment]]. Talon also acted as an able pilot, knowing how to operate starships and a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|When designing the Twi'lek I was attempting to call to mind a sci-fantasy version of a female Pict warrior—tattooed and fierce in appearance. Perhaps she is someone who uses their tribal markings as their armor—as if through some 'magic' she does not need actual armor.|Jan Duursema|{{JCFCite|url=posts/23061440|board=Literature|thread=The Official Legacy Series Discussion Thread|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-02-03|time=4:27 PM|archivedate=20200612214825}}}} Darth Talon was created by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]] as a villain in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]]. She was a prominent part of ''Legacy''{{'s}} marketing, with an image of her used as the cover of the series' [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|Issue 0]][[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] and as one of three pictures included in the series' [[February]] [[2006]] press release.{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?p=118603#p118603|thread=What John and Jan have been up to…|user=[[Randy Stradley]]|date=2006-02-01|time=8:47 AM}} In [[2007]] she was featured on a large banner at [[Dark Horse Comics]]'s booth at ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Celebration IV]],{{DarkHorse|url=Press-Releases/1440/Pictures-and-video-from-Star-Wars-Celebration-IV-6-4-07|text=Pictures and video from Star Wars Celebration IV 6/4/07}}{{Flickr|url=photos/riffsyphon1024/1210425015/in/photostream/?rb=1|text=Darth Talon Strikes the Dark Horse Booth|author=riffsyphon1024|date=2007-05-26|archivedate=20140411162616}} and in [[2008]] she adorned the cover of [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Legacy 0½'']], a followup to Issue 0.[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] While initially designing Talon's appearance, Duursema was attempting to create a tattooed and fierce science fiction/fantasy version of a female [[Wikipedia:Picts|Pict warrior]], intending for her bare skin to invoke a primitive, rather than exploitative, quality. [[File:Darth_Talon_outfit_concepts.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Concept art of various possible outfits for Darth Talon in ''Battle of the Sith Lords'']] Talon played a recurring role as a major antagonist throughout ''Legacy''{{'s}} fifty-issue run[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]][[Legacy (2006) 50|''Legacy'' (2006) 50]] and its followup miniseries, [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']].[[Legacy — War 1|''Legacy — War'' 1]] A fan-favorite character,{{WebCite|url=https://www.thathashtagshow.com/2020/11/12/darth-maul-and-darth-talon-in-the-star-wars-sequels-what-could-have-been|text=Darth Maul and Darth Talon in the Star Wars Sequels? (What could have been)|author=JJ Goodman|date=2020-11-12|work=That Hashtag Show|archivedate=20201113072348}} she was immortalized as a plastic figure four times: in 2008 as a [[Wizards of the Coast]] miniature figurine{{SWMiniCite|set=Legacy of the Force|pack=Darth Talon}} and a 3¾ inch [[Hasbro]] action figure,{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=#04: Darth Talon & Cade Skywalker (''Legacy'' #2)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLCcomicpacks04.asp}} and in [[2010]] as a [[Sideshow Collectibles]] Premium Format Figure{{SideshowCite|set=Premium Format Figures|pack=Darth Talon|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/ssc14darthtalon.asp}} and a [[Gentle Giant Studios|Gentle Giant]] mini bust.{{GentleGiantCite|set=Mini Busts|pack=Darth Talon|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/gg-darthtalon.asp}} She was consistenty popular with ''Star Wars'' cosplayers,{{WebCite|url=http://tosche-station.net/cosplay-week-darth-talon-and-cade-skywalker|text=Cosplay Week: Darth Talon and Cade Skywalker|author=Bria|date=2012-09-12|work=Tosche Station|archivedate=20140413150809}}{{WebCite|url=http://www.geekybytes.org/heres-a-stunning-darth-talon-cosplay|text=Here's A Stunning Darth Talon Cosplay|author=Advait Panchal|date=2013-11-06|work=Geeky Bytes|archivedate=20140413150956}}{{WebCite|url=http://cosplayidol.otakuhouse.com/2013/latin-american/mariana-medellin-darth-talon-from-the-star-wars-legacy|text=Mariana Medellin: Darth Talon from the Star Wars: Legacy Cosplay|work=Otaku House Cosplay Idol 2013|archivedate=20130821124641}} and in [[2009]] a "Drawing Darth Talon" page was highlighted on [[StarWars.com]]'s kids section.{{SWArchive|url=kids/do/draw/f20090813/index.html|text=Drawing Darth Talon}} [[2012|Three years later]], a piece of artwork by [[Mark McKenna]] called "Duel of the Twi'leks" was showcased on the site—featuring Talon dueling [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]]-era [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]], the art was later made available for sale at [[Celebration VI]].{{SWArchive|url=news/star_wars_celebration_6_artists_1.html|text=First 10 Celebration VI Artwork Prints Revealed}} In broader popular culture, Talon's Sideshow Collectibles bust can be seen in the bedroom of [[Wikipedia:Howard Wolowitz|Howard Wolowitz]] in a [[w:c:bigbangtheory:The_Hawking_Excitation|2012 episode]] of [[Wikipedia:The Big Bang Theory|''The Big Bang Theory'']], and she was cited as an inspiration by Sariah Gallego, a young girl who became an internet celebrity in [[2011]] for swearing allegiance to the dark side at [[Disneyland]]'s [[Jedi Training Academy]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.geeksaresexy.net/2011/04/25/sariah-gallego-the-eight-year-old-sith-lord-wholl-kill-darth-vader|text=Meet Sariah Gallego: The Eight-Year-Old Sith Lord Who'll Kill Darth Vader|work=Geeks are Sexy|archivedate=20130518134032}} Talon was set to appear in a [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]-themed [[Star Wars video games|video game]] code-named ''[[Battle of the Sith Lords]].'' Her involvement reportedly proposed by [[George Lucas]] himself who liked the idea of Maul and Talon being friends who play off of each other and likened her character to '40s actress [[Wikipedia:Lauren_Bacall|Lauren Bacall]]. When the developers noted that the characters can't know each other because of the time divide, Lucas changed the main character's identity into [[Maul's descendant]] or clone.{{WebCite|url=https://www.gameinformer.com/b/features/archive/2015/10/26/the-story-of-the-darth-maul-game-that-never-came-to-be.aspx|text=The Story Of The Darth Maul Game That Never Came To Be|author=Reiner, Andrew|date=2015-10-26|work=GameInformer|archivedate=20200320000537}} In the game, Talon would have allied with Maul's descendant to face Darth Krayt's empire, but the project was canceled by [[LucasArts]] in 2011.{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/articles/2014/04/08/lucasarts-canceled-a-star-wars-darth-maul-game|text=LucasArts Canceled a Darth Maul Star Wars Game|author=Mitch Dyer|date=2014-04-08|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|IGN]]|archivedate=20140409011329}} [[Wikipedia:Game Informer|''Game Informer'']] magazine revealed several pieces of concept art for the game in [[2014]], including an ensemble shot of Maul, Talon, [[Savage Opress/Legends|Savage Opress]], and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. Although Talon's character design was identical to her ''Legacy'' appearance, other concept art depicted a suggested redesign of Darth Krayt and an officer of the "New Empire."{{WebCite|url=http://www.gameinformer.com/b/features/archive/2014/04/09/art-from-the-darth-maul-game-you-39-ll-never-get-to-play.aspx|text=Art From The Darth Maul Game You'll Never Get To Play|author=Andrew Reiner|date=[[April 9]], [[2014]], 01:38 PM|work=[[Wikipedia:Game Informer|Game Informer]]|archivedate=20140411123528}} [[File:Darth_Talon_Force_Awakens_Concept_Art.jpg|thumb|272x272px|Concept art of Darth Talon created for ''Star Wars: The Force Awakens'']] George Lucas originally planned to feature the duo of Darth Maul and Darth Talon as the main villains of the ''Star Wars'' [[sequel trilogy]],{{Twitter|kershed|status/1326134062457688069|Paul Duncan|quote=George Lucas' plan for the sequel trilogy included: Darth Maul and Darth Talon as the villains; Luke rebuilding the Jedi Order from survivors of Order 66; Leia forming the New Republic, becoming the Chosen One. More in The Star Wars Archives Ep I-III: https://youtu.be/Ocm__ZQTZVk.|archivedate=20201110120818}} an idea he developed while preparing the films in 2012 before selling [[Lucasfilm]] to [[The Walt Disney Company]].{{Twitter|kershed|status/1326138689144107009|Paul Duncan|quote=These were some of his ideas when prepping the films in 2012, before he sold to Disney, as related to me by George in an interview last year.|archivedate=20201110122856}} Talon was written as the aged Maul's apprentice, with Lucas later saying "She was the new Darth Vader, and most of the action was with her." Early story treatments had her corrupting the son of [[Han Solo]] and [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]],{{WordPress|subdomain=naboonews|url=2018/01/04/george-lucas-wanted-darth-talon-to-corrupt-han-and-leias-son-in-episode-vii|name=Naboo News|text=George Lucas wanted Darth Talon to corrupt Han and Leia's son in Episode VII|date=2018-01-04|archivedate=20200317181103}} but after [[J.J. Abrams|J. J. Abrams]] started to work on the project, her character later evolved into a "Jedi Killer" that ultimately became the character of [[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren]].''[[The Art of Star Wars: The Force Awakens]]''''[[Star Wars: Fascinating Facts]]'' According to [[Pablo Hidalgo]] of the [[Lucasfilm Story Group]], "The Jedi Killer morphed from Talon corrupting the son to becoming the son." Lucasfilm's Creative Art Manager [[Phil Szostak]] has called Talon "just one stop of many on the road to Kylo Ren" and presumed that Talon's place in [[Star Wars: Episode VII The Force Awakens|Episode VII's]] story was not significantly developed, with her originally in consideration because of admiration for her character design, although he admitted he was only involved in the design development and not the story.{{Twitter|PhilSzostak|status/1038895185147527168|[[Phil Szostak]]|quote=If I remember right, it was simply Darth Talon's look that was admired and therefore considered. Very unlikely that she would have been the same EU character and her place in Ep7's story was not thought all the way through. She was just one stop of many on the road to Kylo Ren.|archivedate=20200813210416}} Concept art of her can be seen in [[2015]]'s ''[[The Art of Star Wars: The Force Awakens]]'', which includes an [[Iain McCaig]] drawing of Talon wielding dual red lightsabers and standing in the embrace of a mysterious blue-skinned [[Alien/Legends|alien]] nicknamed "Uber". Also included are storyboards of Talon getting harassed in a bar, only for a [[Jedi Master]] to save her and then sleep with her, before being murdered by her in his sleep. According to author [[Paul Duncan]], while writing his book ''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes I–III, 1999–2005]],'' in which Lucas' original plans for the sequel trilogy discussed, one interviewee mentioned singer and actress [[Wikipedia:Rihanna#Selected_filmography|Rihanna]] as a possible casting or model for McCaig's art.{{Twitter|kershed|status/1654490255075024896|[[Paul Duncan]]|quote=Somewhere it was suggested that @rihanna would have been good casting for Darth Talon.|archivedate=20230604123914}} In 2021, Talon was added the the sandbox mobile game ''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]''{{EA|subdomain=forums.galaxy-of-heroes.starwars|url=discussion/251783/developer-insights-darth-talon|text=Developer Insights: Darth Talon}} as part of celebrations for [[Lucasfilm]]'s 50th anniversary alongside other Legends characters like [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] and [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]].{{EA|subdomain=forums.galaxy-of-heroes.starwars|url=discussion/251416/lucasfilm-50th-anniversary-and-upcoming-legends-characters|text=Lucasfilm 50th Anniversary and Upcoming Legends Characters}} Due to her typically revealing depictions, she was given a more family-friendly costume due to the target audience of the game.{{YouTube|video=HqR1oIcbYlU|text=Darth Talon: Exclusive Insight Interview with Developers|channel=UC_2rMVChBM3UjgDQ9O1vKaw|name=Reality Skewed Gamers – RSG}} Her updated costume resulted in her chest covered by a sleeveless crop-top, long gloves to her upper arm and armored leggings covering her legs from the waist down. In addition to the visual change, Talon was also given close synergy with the [[Sith Triumvirate]] from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' due to the lack of Krayt or other characters from the One Sith empire. Talon is the subject of two continuity errors in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]]—in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 19|nineteenth issue]], she is impaled by Cade Skywalker's lightsaber, which is said to have killed her in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. She is alive and well as early as [[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]], however,[[Legacy (2006) 22|''Legacy'' (2006) 22]] and [[Legacy (2006) 23|''Legacy'' (2006) 23]] shows her recovering from the impalement in a bacta tank. Additionally, ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 2|second issue]] has Talon ascend to the rank of Sith Hand in the year 137 ABY, but the sourcebook [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] incorrectly states that she is already a Hand seven years earlier.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Darth Talon}} *[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] {{Co}} *[[Legacy
